sections.subtitle: Team Form Overview
sections.text: KVC Westerlo are struggling with form, winless in their last 3 matches (1 draw, 2 losses), though they showed attacking intent with high shot counts. KRC Genk, on the other hand, are unbeaten in their last 5 matches (2 wins, 3 draws), displaying resilience and consistency. This gives Genk a slight edge going into the match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, KRC Genk have dominated, winning both matches (1-0 and 2-1). Genk have also not lost to Westerlo in their last 7 meetings, often scoring first and controlling the first half, as seen in 5 of the last 7 games.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics Insight
sections.text: Westerlo have scored 19 goals this season (5th in the league) but struggle defensively, conceding 21 (14th). Genk have a balanced record with 17 goals scored and conceded, and their ability to create chances (evident from high possession and final third entries) could exploit Westerlo's defensive frailties.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors and Match Outlook
sections.text: Westerlo miss key defender Tuur Rommens, which could weaken their backline further. Genk's unbeaten streak and historical dominance over Westerlo, combined with Sofascore's 42% away win probability, tilt the scales in their favor. Expect a competitive match with Genk likely to capitalize on their momentum.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, KRC Genk leads KVC Westerlo 1-0 at Het Kuipje. The away side struck through Hyun-gyu Oh in the 35th minute, capitalizing on a solid attacking move. Genk has dominated possession and shown more intent in the final third, while Westerlo has struggled to create clear-cut opportunities despite some moments of promise. Both teams have had their share of entries into dangerous areas, but Genk’s clinical edge has made the difference so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: KRC Genk has controlled the game with significant ball possession and a higher number of final third entries. Their ability to create big chances has been evident, even if they’ve missed a couple of key opportunities. Defensively, they’ve been stable, limiting Westerlo’s threat in front of goal. On the other hand, KVC Westerlo has shown resilience with a decent number of penalty area entries and possessions won in the final third. However, their lack of shots on target and inability to convert pressure into big chances highlight a blunt attacking force. Defensively, they’ve held up reasonably well but were caught out for Genk’s goal.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players at the break
halftimeSections.text: For KRC Genk, Hyun-gyu Oh has been the standout performer, finding the net and posing a constant threat up front. Konstantinos Karetsas also played a pivotal role with a crucial assist, showcasing creativity in midfield. Bryan Heynen’s key passes have kept Genk’s attacks ticking. For KVC Westerlo, players like Doğucan Haspolat have tried to spark something with key passes and a presence in midfield. Allahyar Sayyadmanesh and Arthur Piedfort have contributed with some attacking intent, but overall, the home side lacks a cutting edge in the final third.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw momentum ebb and flow between the two sides. Genk started brightly, establishing early control with consistent attacking spells in the opening minutes. Westerlo responded with a strong period of pressure around the 10-minute mark, pushing forward with intent. However, Genk regained the upper hand midway through the half, building sustained attacks that culminated in their goal. Towards the end of the half, momentum swung back and forth, with both teams having their moments, though Genk looked more dangerous overall.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, KRC Genk will likely aim to maintain their dominance in possession and exploit any defensive lapses from Westerlo. Their attacking intent suggests they could add to their tally if they remain clinical. KVC Westerlo, playing at home, will need to push harder for an equalizer, likely taking more risks and committing players forward. This could open up spaces for Genk to counter, but it might also give Westerlo a chance to capitalize if they can sharpen their finishing. Tactical adjustments from the home side will be crucial to turn their pressure into tangible results.
